title: Activating Mutations in the Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor Underlying Responsiveness of Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer to Gefitinib
abstract: The identification of mutations in the EGFR kinase domain in patients with NSCLC who had dramatic responses to gefitinib established the molecular basis for targeted therapy in lung cancer.
keyFinding: Identified EGFR kinase domain mutations (L858R, exon 19 del) as the molecular basis for gefitinib sensitivity in NSCLC — the paper that started precision oncology in lung cancer.

title: Gefitinib or Carboplatin–Paclitaxel in Pulmonary Adenocarcinoma (IPASS)
abstract: The IPASS trial demonstrated that in Asian patients with lung adenocarcinoma, gefitinib was superior to carboplatin-paclitaxel — but only in EGFR-mutation-positive patients, definitively establishing EGFR mutation status as a predictive biomarker.
keyFinding: Proved EGFR mutation testing is mandatory before TKI therapy. EGFR-positive patients: 71% response to gefitinib vs 47% chemo. EGFR-negative: gefitinib was inferior.

title: Acquired EGFR C797S Mutation Mediates Resistance to AZD9291 in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Harboring EGFR T790M
abstract: First identification of C797S as the mechanism of acquired resistance to osimertinib (AZD9291) in patients with T790M-positive NSCLC, discovered through plasma ctDNA sequencing.
keyFinding: C797S abolishes osimertinib's covalent binding to Cys797. Detected in ctDNA before tumor progression — established liquid biopsy as a resistance monitoring tool.

title: Overall Survival with Osimertinib in Untreated, EGFR-Mutated Advanced NSCLC (FLAURA)
abstract: Final overall survival results from the FLAURA trial showing osimertinib achieved a median OS of 38.6 months vs 31.8 months for comparator EGFR-TKIs in first-line EGFR-mutant NSCLC.
keyFinding: Osimertinib: 38.6 mo median OS vs 31.8 mo for gefitinib/erlotinib. Established osimertinib as standard first-line therapy. Also showed superior CNS penetration and activity.

title: Osimertinib in Untreated EGFR-Mutated Advanced Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer (FLAURA PFS)
abstract: Primary PFS results from FLAURA establishing osimertinib superiority in first-line EGFR-mutant NSCLC with median PFS of 18.9 vs 10.2 months.
keyFinding: Osimertinib PFS: 18.9 months vs 10.2 months for comparator (HR 0.46). CNS metastasis subgroup showed even larger benefit. Led to FDA approval as first-line standard of care.

title: Osimertinib Plus Chemotherapy in EGFR-Mutated Advanced NSCLC (FLAURA2)
abstract: FLAURA2 showed osimertinib plus platinum-based chemotherapy significantly improved PFS over osimertinib alone in first-line EGFR-mutant NSCLC, median 25.5 vs 16.7 months.
keyFinding: Combination of osimertinib + chemo: PFS 25.5 vs 16.7 months (HR 0.62). Raises question of whether all patients need combination or a biomarker-selected subgroup.

title: AZD9291, an Irreversible EGFR TKI, Overcomes T790M-Mediated Resistance to EGFR Inhibitors in Lung Cancer
abstract: Preclinical characterization of osimertinib (AZD9291) as a mutant-selective, irreversible EGFR inhibitor that overcomes T790M resistance while sparing wild-type EGFR.
keyFinding: Osimertinib exploits Cys797 for covalent bond formation. ~200x selectivity for T790M mutant over wild-type EGFR explains its superior tolerability vs. afatinib/dacomitinib.

title: Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor Mutations in Lung Cancer
abstract: Comprehensive review of EGFR mutations in lung cancer, covering the structural basis of oncogenic activation, mechanisms of sensitivity to TKIs, and early resistance data.
keyFinding: Explains structural basis of why exon 19 deletions and L858R confer sensitivity: both shift kinase to active conformation that binds ATP competitively more weakly than inhibitors.

title: Resistance Mechanisms to Osimertinib in EGFR-Mutated Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
abstract: Systematic review of all known mechanisms of acquired resistance to osimertinib: on-target (C797S, T790M loss), bypass (MET amp, HER2, RAS), and histologic transformation.
keyFinding: C797S occurs in ~15% of first-line osimertinib progressors. MET amplification (~15%), KRAS mutations (~5%), SCLC transformation (~5%). Most patients have multiple co-occurring resistance mechanisms.

title: Discovery of Potent and Selective EGFR Degraders by Coupling PROTACs with Affinity-Selection Mass Spectrometry
abstract: Discovery of potent EGFR-targeting PROTACs that degrade EGFR regardless of C797S mutation status, overcoming the fundamental limitation of active-site inhibitors.
keyFinding: PROTAC degraders maintain activity against C797S because they don't require kinase activity — they recruit E3 ligase to EGFR surface outside the ATP-binding site.

title: PANDORA Study: Patient-Reported Outcomes and ICU Delirium in Critical Illness Survivors
journal: Annals of the American Thoracic Society
abstract: The PANDORA study evaluated long-term patient-reported outcomes, ICU delirium burden, and post-intensive care syndrome in survivors at Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center.
dibakarNotes: My co-authored publication from my BIDMC internship. This study followed ICU survivors longitudinally to characterize delirium frequency, duration, and its correlation with long-term cognitive and functional outcomes as part of the PICS (Post-Intensive Care Syndrome) framework.
keyFinding: Characterized ICU delirium patterns and PROs in critical illness survivors — contributes to evidence base for PICS intervention design.

title: Molecular Mechanisms of Cardiac Fibrosis: Transcriptomics and Single-Cell Approaches
abstract: Review of molecular mechanisms driving cardiac fibrosis with emphasis on single-cell transcriptomic approaches that have revealed fibroblast heterogeneity and activation states.
dibakarNotes: Highly relevant to my work in the BCH Choudhury Lab. The scRNA-seq approaches described here are directly applicable to our cardiac fibrosis transcriptomics pipeline.
keyFinding: Single-cell transcriptomics resolved cardiac fibroblast into multiple activation states — myofibroblasts, matrifibrocytes, and cycling fibroblasts — each with distinct gene expression signatures.

title: Co-Occurring Genomic Alterations Define Major Subgroups of KRAS-Mutant Lung Adenocarcinoma with Distinct Therapeutic Vulnerabilities
abstract: Large genomic study defining KRAS co-mutation subgroups (KRAS/STK11, KRAS/KEAP1, KRAS/TP53) with distinct immune landscapes and treatment responses.
keyFinding: STK11/LKB1 co-mutation with KRAS predicts primary resistance to immunotherapy — distinct from KRAS/TP53 which responds well. Framework for co-mutation subgrouping applicable to EGFR resistance analysis.

title: Co-occurring Alterations in the RAS-MAPK Pathway Limit Response to MET Inhibitor Treatment in MET Exon 14 Skipping Mutation-Positive Lung Cancer
abstract: Review of mechanisms by which co-occurring genetic alterations limit therapeutic response in oncogene-driven lung cancers, with implications for combination therapy strategies.
keyFinding: Co-occurring RAS-MAPK alterations limit response to targeted therapy by providing bypass signaling — argues for upfront combination treatment in molecularly complex tumors.

title: CFT8919, a Bifunctional EGFR Degrader, Overcomes C797S Resistance in EGFR-Mutant NSCLC
abstract: Preclinical and early clinical data on CFT8919, a PROTAC-based EGFR degrader being developed to overcome C797S and T790M compound resistance in NSCLC.
keyFinding: CFT8919 degrades EGFR with exon 19 del or L858R regardless of T790M/C797S status — activity maintained across all resistance configurations that block osimertinib.
